misconceptions.text: Applied the wrong inverse operation when isolating the variable.
misconceptions.hint: After moving constants, divide both sides by the coefficient of x.

misconceptions.text: Added numerators and denominators separately instead of finding a common denominator.
misconceptions.hint: Find the LCD first, convert both fractions, then add only the numerators.

misconceptions.text: Only computed half the formula — forgot to multiply by the full variable value.
misconceptions.hint: Re-read the formula and substitute every variable before calculating.

misconceptions.text: The student confused squaring a variable with multiplying it by two.
misconceptions.hint: Remember that squaring a number means multiplying it by itself, not by two.

misconceptions.text: Applied the wrong inverse operation when isolating the variable.
misconceptions.hint: After moving constants, divide both sides by the coefficient of x.

misconceptions.text: Added numerators and denominators separately instead of finding a common denominator.
misconceptions.hint: Find the LCD first, convert both fractions, then add only the numerators.

misconceptions.text: Confused Newton's First Law (inertia) with the Second Law (F = ma).
misconceptions.hint: First Law: objects stay at rest or in motion without net force. Second Law: F = ma.
